title: रामायण चौपाई
devanagariText:
॥ श्री रामायण चौपाई ॥

॥ श्रीरामचरितमानस की प्रमुख चौपाइयाँ ॥

॥ बालकाण्ड ॥

श्रीरामचन्द्र कृपालु भजु मन हरण भवभय दारुणम्।
नवकञ्ज लोचन कंज मुख कर कंज पद कंजारुणम्॥

कन्दर्प अगणित अमित छबि नव नील नीरज सुन्दरम्।
पट पीत मानहुँ तड़ित रुचि शुचि नौमि जनक सुतावरम्॥

भजु दीनबन्धु दिनेश दानव दैत्यवंशनिकन्दनम्।
रघुनन्द आनन्दकन्द कोसलचन्द दशरथनन्दनम्॥

सिर मुकुट कुण्डल तिलक चारु उदारु अंग विभूषणम्।
आजानुभुज शर चाप धर संग्राम जित खरदूषणम्॥

॥ अयोध्याकाण्ड ॥

मंगल भवन अमंगल हारी। द्रवउ सो दशरथ अजिर बिहारी॥
हरषित महतारी मुनि देखी। बोले मधुर बचन सबिसेषी॥

राम सो बड़भागी सेवकाई। जो राम रजनीचर मारहीं॥
सो कुल धन्य उमा सुनु सोई। रामचरन दृढ़ प्रीति न जोई॥

जासु नाम भव औषध मूला। तिनहिं लाग संसार बभूला॥

॥ अरण्यकाण्ड ॥

कामहि नारि पियारि जिमि लोभहि प्रिय जिमि दाम।
तिमि रघुनाथ निरन्तर प्रिय लागहु मोहि राम॥

एक भरोसो एक बल एक आस बिस्वास।
राम रूप स्वाती जलद चातक तुलसीदास॥

॥ किष्किन्धाकाण्ड ॥

नहिं दरिद्र सम दुख जग माहीं। सब ते कठिन सुमिरत मन नाहीं॥
जेहि कर मन रघुनाथ के पासा। कहा बिगरि तेहि कर लेखा॥

॥ सुन्दरकाण्ड ॥

मन क्रम बचन छाड़ि चतुराई। भजिअ सो एक राम रघुराई॥
जाके अरि मित्र सम जानहीं। तजि सकल भजे रघुबीरहीं॥

॥ लंकाकाण्ड ॥

सुनहु भरत भावी प्रबल बिलखि कहेउ मुनि नाथ।
हानि लाभ जीवन मरण जस अपजस बिधि हाथ॥

राम ब्रह्म परमारथ रूपा। अबिगत अलख अनादि अनूपा॥
सकल बिकार रहित गतभेदा। कहि नित नेति निरूपहिं बेदा॥

॥ उत्तरकाण्ड ॥

बिनु सत्संग बिबेक न होई। राम कृपा बिनु सुलभ न सोई॥
सत्संगत मुद मंगल मूला। सोई फल सिधि सब साधन फूला॥

भगत कामना ते कामधेनु। भवबारिधि बोहित हरिनामा॥
राम नाम जपि जीहा जानी। जो सुख चहसि अखण्ड अमानी॥

मो सम कौन कुटिल खल कामी। जो सब भाँति भजें मम स्वामी॥
उमा राम बिनु जतन अपारा। मिटहि न जीवन केर अँधारा॥

॥ इति रामायण चौपाई सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ramayana Chopai ||

|| Shri Ramcharitmanas ki Pramukh Chaupaiyan ||

|| Balkand ||

Shri Ramchandra kripalu bhaju man haran bhavabhay darunam.
Nava-kanja lochan kanj mukh kar kanj pad kanjarunam.

Kandarpa aganit amit chhabi nav neel niraj sundaram.
Pat pit manahin tadit ruchi shuchi naumi Janak-suta-varam.

Bhaju Dinbandhu Dinesh danav daitya-vansha-nikandanam.
Raghunand Anandakand Kosalachand Dasharathnandanam.

Sir mukut kundal tilak charu udaru ang vibhushanam.
Ajanubhuj shar chap dhar sangram jit Khardushanam.

|| Ayodhyakand ||

Mangal bhavan amangal hari. Dravau so Dashrath ajir bihari.
Harshit mahtari muni dekhi. Bole madhur bachan sabiseshi.

Ram so badbhagi sevakai. Jo Ram rajnichar maraheen.
So kul dhanya Uma sunu soi. Ramcharan dridh priti na joi.

Jasu nam bhav aushadh mula. Tinahi lag sansar babhula.

|| Aranyakand ||

Kamahi nari piyari jimi lobhahi priya jimi dam.
Timi Raghunath nirantar priya lagahu mohi Ram.

Ek bharoso ek bal ek aas biswas.
Ram rup Swati jalad chatak Tulsidas.

|| Kishkindhakand ||

Nahi daridr sam dukh jag mahin. Sab te kathin sumirat man nahin.
Jehi kar man Raghunath ke pasa. Kaha bigari tehi kar lekha.

|| Sundarkand ||

Man kram bachan chhadi chaturai. Bhajia so ek Ram Raghurai.
Jake ari mitra sam janhin. Taji sakal bhaje Raghubirhin.

|| Lankakand ||

Sunahu Bharat bhavi prabal bilakhi kaheu muni nath.
Hani labh jivan maran jas apjas bidhi hath.

Ram Brahm parmarath rupa. Abigat alakh anadi anupa.
Sakal bikar rahit gatbheda. Kahi nit neti nirupahi Beda.

|| Uttarkand ||

Binu satsang bibek na hoi. Ram kripa binu sulabh na soi.
Satsangat mud mangal mula. Soi phal sidhi sab sadhan phula.

Bhagat kamna te Kamdhenu. Bhavbaridhi bohit Harinama.
Ram nam japi jiha jani. Jo sukh chahsi akhand amani.

Mo sam kaun kutit khal kami. Jo sab bhanti bhajen mama swami.
Uma Ram binu jatan apara. Mitahi na jivan ker andhara.

|| Iti Ramayana Chopai Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
बालकाण्ड: मन से श्रीरामचन्द्र को भजो जो कृपालु हैं, भवभय को हरने वाले हैं। नवकमल जैसे नेत्र, कमल जैसा मुख, कमल जैसे हाथ और अरुण कमल जैसे चरण हैं। करोड़ों कामदेव की छवि वाले, नील कमल जैसे सुन्दर, पीताम्बर पहने, जानकी वर को मैं नमन करता हूँ।

दीनबन्धु, दैत्यवंश का निर्दलन करने वाले, रघुनन्द, आनन्दकन्द, कोसलचन्द, दशरथनन्दन को भजो।

अयोध्याकाण्ड: मंगल भवन अमंगल हारी - जो मंगल के निवास और अमंगल के नाशक हैं, दशरथ के आँगन में विहार करने वाले वे द्रवित हों।

अरण्यकाण्ड: जैसे कामी को नारी प्रिय है, लोभी को धन प्रिय है, वैसे ही हे रघुनाथ! आप मुझे निरन्तर प्रिय लगें। एक भरोसा, एक बल, एक आशा, एक विश्वास - राम रूपी स्वाती बूँद के चातक तुलसीदास।

उत्तरकाण्ड: बिना सत्संग विवेक नहीं होता, राम कृपा बिना वह सुलभ नहीं। सत्संगति आनन्द और मंगल का मूल है। राम ब्रह्म हैं, परमार्थ रूप, अविगत, अलख, अनादि, अनूपम। वेद नित्य 'नेति-नेति' कहकर उनका निरूपण करते हैं।
benefits: Encapsulates the essence of the entire Ramcharitmanas
Invokes the fruit of reading the full ramayana in condensed form
Invokes the blessing of spiritual wisdom and devotion to lord rama
Traditionally recited for removal of ignorance, sorrow, and fear
Ideal for daily recitation when time is limited
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Rama, Ramayana Chopai (रामायण चौपाई) stands as a sacred prayer. This sacred composition is sourced from Ramcharitmanas by Goswami Tulsidas and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to grant the fruit of reading the full Ramayana in condensed form, bestow spiritual wisdom and devotion to Lord Rama, and provide guidance for righteous living. It is especially recommended on Sunday (Ravivar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Ram Navami and Vivah Panchami for maximum spiritual benefit. At 20 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 15 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Ramayana Chopai in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read key Ramayana Chopai from Ramcharitmanas by Tulsidas in Hindi with transliteration. Essential verses from all seven Kands.

seoFaqs.question: What are the Ramayana Chaupai by Tulsidas?
seoFaqs.answer: Ramayana Chaupai (रामायण चौपाई) is a curated selection of the most revered chaupais (four-line couplets) from Sri Ramcharitmanas — the 16th-century Awadhi-Hindi epic composed by Goswami Tulsidas (1532–1623 CE) at Varanasi and Ayodhya. The Ramcharitmanas contains over 12,800 verses across seven kands (Bal, Ayodhya, Aranya, Kishkindha, Sundar, Lanka, Uttar) primarily in chaupai-doha-soratha meters. Devotees across North India select powerful chaupais — Mangal Bhavan Amangal Hari, Sri Ramchandra Kripalu Bhajman, Hoihi Soi Jo Ram Rachi Rakha — for daily recitation as Tulsi-mantra, jeevan-samasya remedies, and parayan path. The chaupai collection is preserved in classical Tulsidas tradition. (Source: Sri Ramcharitmanas — Goswami Tulsidas.)

seoFaqs.question: Who was Goswami Tulsidas and why is Ramcharitmanas so revered?
seoFaqs.answer: Goswami Tulsidas (1532–1623 CE) was the great medieval bhakti saint-poet born in Rajapur (Banda district, UP) who is regarded as an incarnation of Maharshi Valmiki. He composed twelve major works including Ramcharitmanas (his magnum opus, completed at Ayodhya in 1574 CE), Vinaya Patrika, Hanuman Chalisa, Dohavali, Kavitavali, Geetavali, Janaki Mangal, Parvati Mangal, Ramlala Nahachhu, Ramagya Prashna, Vairagya Sandipani, and Krishna Gitavali. Ramcharitmanas — written in Awadhi for the common devotee rather than Sanskrit-knowing Brahmins — democratized Rama-bhakti, and is recited in millions of Hindu homes daily through nau-paath (nine-day parayan), nava-aha (continuous recitation), and akhand path. The Manas is venerated as the fifth Veda of Kali Yuga and the most popular scripture of North India. (Sources: Bhaktamala by Nabhadas; Tulsidas hagiography.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Mangal Bhavan Amangal Hari chaupai?
seoFaqs.answer: Mangal Bhavan Amangal Hari, Umaa Sahit Jehi Japat Purari (मंगल भवन अमंगल हारी, उमा सहित जेहि जपत पुरारी) is from the Balkand of Ramcharitmanas and is one of the most chanted Tulsidas chaupais of all time. Translation: That Lord Sri Rama who is the very abode of auspiciousness (mangal-bhavan) and the destroyer of all inauspiciousness (amangal-hari), whom Lord Shiva (Purari — slayer of Tripurasura) along with Mata Parvati (Uma) constantly chants — to that Sri Rama I bow. The chaupai is recited at the start of any auspicious work, parayan, marriage, business inauguration, and during distress to invoke Rama's mangal-protection. Tulsidas placed it strategically to invoke divine sanction at the very threshold of the Manas narrative. (Source: Sri Ramcharitmanas — Balkand mangalacharan.)

seoFaqs.question: Which Ramayana chaupais are best for specific life problems?
seoFaqs.answer: Tulsidas tradition prescribes specific chaupais as siddha-mantras for life problems: (1) For obstacles — Mangal Bhavan Amangal Hari (Balkand). (2) For protection — Hoihi Soi Jo Ram Rachi Rakha (Ayodhya kand — surrender chaupai). (3) For wealth — Jaasu Kripa Aja Shiv Sanakaadi (Balkand). (4) For court cases — Bandau Guru Pad Padum Paragaa (opening). (5) For Hanuman bala — Atulit Bal Dhamam (Sundarkand). (6) For removing fear — Bhay Bin Hoi Na Preeti (Sundarkand — Vibhishan-Ravan). (7) For marriage — Tab Janak Pai Brahma Sandesa (Balkand — Sita-Rama vivah). (8) For illness — Daihik Daivik Bhautik Tapa (Balkand — three-fold suffering). (9) For knowledge — Jaasu Naam Bhav Bheshaj. Each is recited 11/108 times daily for 41-day anushthan. (Sources: Tulsi Ramayan Mahima; Manas Bhushan tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How should Ramayana chaupai parayan be performed at home?
seoFaqs.answer: Ramcharitmanas parayan is performed in several formats. (1) Akhand Path — non-stop recitation completing in approximately 24 hours, with multiple readers in shifts. (2) Nau-Paath — nine-day parayan covering one kand per day plus mangalacharan. (3) Maas Paath — completing in one month with daily 1-2 hour reading. (4) Saptah-Paath — seven-day intensive. The selected chaupai recitation is performed daily morning after snan, facing east, before a Rama-Sita-Lakshman-Hanuman picture, with Tulsi mala. Begin with Sri Ganesha invocation, Saraswati vandana, Tulsidas pranam, then chaupais 11 or 108 times each, followed by aarti and prasad distribution. Best on Tuesday-Saturday (Hanuman days), Ram Navami (Chaitra Shukla 9), Vivah Panchami (Margashirsha Shukla 5 — Rama-Sita marriage), Akshay Tritiya, and during Shravan month. (Sources: Tulsi Ghat paddhati Varanasi; Manas Mandali Ayodhya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Ramayana Chaupai different from Hanuman Chalisa and Sundar Kand path?
seoFaqs.answer: All three are Tulsidas compositions but differ in scope. (1) Hanuman Chalisa — 40-verse standalone hymn entirely to Hanuman, recited daily for protection, courage, planetary remedy (especially Saturn-Mangal). (2) Sundarkand — the entire fifth book of Ramcharitmanas (about 525 verses) describing Hanuman's Lanka journey, recited as a complete book on Tuesdays-Saturdays for obstacle removal and wish-fulfilment. (3) Ramayana Chaupai — a curated devotional anthology of the most powerful Manas chaupais selected across all seven kands, allowing devotees to recite the essence of Manas in 15-30 minutes daily. The chaupai selection is ideal for those who cannot do full Manas parayan but want daily Rama-Manas-bhakti. All three are complementary — Hanuman Chalisa for Hanuman, Sundarkand for Hanuman-leela, Ramayana Chaupai for the comprehensive Rama-darshan. (Sources: Tulsi Peetha Chitrakoot; Sankat Mochan Varanasi tradition.)
